candidates.why: Separate operating D&A line above operating profit, so not embedded in the selected COGS row 'Materials and services'; however it is not labelled as COGS/cost-of-sales specific, so assigning it to COGS would be speculative.
Separate operating D&A line above operating profit and outside 'Materials and services', but not identified as COGS-related.
Operating depreciation line above EBIT cutoff, but not labelled as belonging to cost of sales or materials/services; could overlap with broader depreciation disclosure.

variables.notes: No D&A row is explicitly tied to COGS / cost of sales / cost of revenues. The selected COGS bucket is only 'Materials and services'. Visible D&A rows are separate operating lines but appear company-wide rather than COGS-specific, so adding any of them to COGS risks misclassification and double counting.

candidates.why: Separate operating D&A line at the same level as 'Employee benefit expenses' and 'Other operating expenses', so it appears outside the selected XSGA_COMPONENTS bucket; but it is not explicitly SG&A-specific and may include non-SG&A depreciation.
Separate operating line above operating profit and outside the selected XSGA_COMPONENTS rows, but not labelled as SG&A/admin/R&D-specific.
Separate operating depreciation disclosure, but likely overlaps with the broader depreciation line and is not clearly an SG&A-only amount.

variables.notes: R:c. The selected XSGA_COMPONENTS bucket ('Employee benefit expenses' + 'Other operating expenses') clearly excludes separate D&A lines. However, the visible D&A disclosures are generic operating amounts, not specifically SG&A/admin/marketing/R&D. Also, 'Depreciation, right-of-use assets' appears to be a component disclosure within depreciation, so selecting components could double count. Because no SG&A-specific D&A row is visible, conservative output is empty, but this bucket warrants manual review if downstream methodology expects generic operating D&A to be assigned to overhead.
